name: Old-Fashioned Cornbread Dressing
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Prepare a 9-inch square pan and a 2-quart casserole dish with cooking spray; set them aside.
3. To prepare cornbread, combine cornmeal, baking powder, whipped egg whites, buttermilk in a mixing bowl. Pour batter evenly into the prepared 9-inch pan.
4. Bake for 20 minutes at 400°F. Reduce heat to 325°F and bake an additional 25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
5. Remove cornbread from the pan and crumble into a mixing bowl.
6. Add onions, celery, sage, poultry seasoning, black pepper, chicken broth, and whipped egg whites to the cornbread crumbs.
7. Loosely spoon stuffing mixture into the casserole dish.
8. Bake for 45 minutes at 325°F until heated through and lightly browned on top.
Per serving: 91 Calories; 1g Fat (7% of calories from fat); 5g Protein; 16g Carbohydrate; 1mg Cholesterol; 263mg Sodium
Notes:
Buttermilk Substitute: Combine 3 tablespoons vinegar with enough milk to equal 1 1/4 cups. Let stand for 5 minutes.
Time Saving Tip: Bake cornbread 1-3 days in advance. Complete the stuffing preparations the day before or morning of dinner, and place casserole in oven 1 hour before serving time.
